TextENGNew: And then, the next day.
The last period of the afternoon was PE. We changed into our gym clothes and headed out to the schoolyard.
The younger students had already assembled there, lined up and waiting.
``Hey, sorry to keep you wait-- huh?``
I started to call out casually as usual, raising my hand in greeting -- but stopped halfway.
The moment they saw our faces, their usual innocent smiles vanished, replaced by expressions brimming with tension and fighting spirit.
``We look forward to this!!``
They turned to face us and shouted in unison.
For a moment, I was nearly overwhelmed by the sheer force of their energy.
``R-right!!``
Refusing to be outdone, I immediately shouted back.
The tremendous fighting spirit radiating from every one of those younger students -- it was a genuine expression of just how badly they wanted to become our comrades. That made me genuinely happy.
``Ohh? Everyone's really fired up!@k@vS14/03/210300085. This is going to be fun.``
``Ahahaha!@k@vS14/02/210200048. They look really tough!@k@vS14/02/210200049. We'll have to work even harder than usual!``
Following us out to the schoolyard, Rena and Mion wore broad smiles as well, having picked up on the younger students' passion.
...But their eyes weren't smiling. Like raptors sizing up prey, they met the younger students' intensity head-on.
The battle had already begun!
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS14/04/210400102. The stronger the opponent, the sweeter the victory!!``
``Houjou... let's see how long that confidence lasts.``
``Oh my?@k@vS14/04/210400104. When did Shion-san's trained lapdogs learn to bark so boldly?``
``Ha ha ha!@k@vS14/25/212500007. Your undefeated record ends today!``
Neither Tomita-kun nor Okamura-kun flinched, maintaining their provocative stance. Unfazed by Satoko's barbs, they rolled their shoulders and flexed through careful warm-up stretches.
Impressive composure... If they join our ranks, with the right training they could definitely become a formidable fighting force!
``Isn't it a bit early for that, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS14/02/210200051. Whether they make the cut or not depends entirely on today.``
Rena casually reined in my runaway enthusiasm.
Well, she had a point. But regardless of who made it in, just the thought of gaining new members had me buzzing with excitement!
``That said, if nobody meets our standards, it's entirely possible we'll end up with zero new members.``
``Precisely!@k@vS14/04/VT4Ba_satoko1012. Now then, let us see just how well our adversaries can entertain us!``
Entrance exam or not, all three of them clearly had zero intention of going easy.
I understood the sentiment, but demanding they perform at our level right from the start seemed harsh... Or was I just being soft?
Just then --
``If their conviction is strong enough, I'm sure they can rise to the challenge.@k@vS14/05/210500082. So I believe meeting them with everything we have is the proper show of respect.``
``...Rika-chan.``
``The taller the mountain, the greater the joy when you finally cross its peak.@k@vS14/05/210500084. So fight on, Keiichi. Fight on.``
As if she'd seen right through my hesitation, Rika-chan offered those words of encouragement. Hearing them lightened the weight on my heart, even if only a little.
...She was right. When someone comes at you with everything they have, the only meaningful response is to answer in kind. Half-hearted kindness would be an insult to their resolve.
``Yeah, you're right!@k@vS14/01/210100058. Then I'll give it everything I've got and more!!``
``Hmm? What's wrong, Rika-chan?``
``...Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500087. Did what you just said... remind you of anything?``
``What I just said...?@k@vS14/01/210100061. What do you mean, Rika-chan?``
``...No, it's nothing.``
For an instant, Rika-chan's face seemed to cloud over with melancholy. But before I could look closer, she spun on her heel and walked away toward Satoko without looking back.
``What's wrong?@k@vS14/02/210200053. Keiichi-kun.``
``Oh, no... it's nothing.``
What was that...? Something prickled deep in my chest, as if something was stirring restlessly inside me...
``All right then, first -- the rules of engagement!@k@vS14/03/210300088. Everyone, stick one of these to your back.``
Mion handed out white numbered bibs to everyone. They were rectangular pieces of white fabric, the kind used at field days.
Rika-chan had apparently prepared in advance -- she carried a box of safety pins and black markers from the classroom.
``Normally you'd call out the name of anyone you spot, but this schoolyard is wide open -- that gives 'it' too much of an advantage.``

``So this time, write whatever number you like on your bib and pin it to your back.@k@vS14/03/210300091. If the seeker reads your number aloud while touching the can with their foot, you're out!@k@vS14/03/210300092. Also--``
Mion continued her explanation while tapping her foot on the circle Rena had efficiently drawn on the ground with chalk -- about one meter in radius.
``If the seeker tags you while touching the can, you're also out!@k@vS14/03/210300094. But the seeker can only stay inside the white circle for five seconds at a time!``
``I see.@k@vS14/01/210100065. ...Wait, couldn't you just call out random numbers then?``
``Umm, in that case...``
``Oh, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300097. Sorry, but could you serve as referee?``
``Referee...?``
Rika-chan, who had been distributing safety pins and markers to the younger students, looked up at Mion with a puzzled expression.

``Right!@k@vS14/03/210300099. Rika-chan will judge whether the seeker correctly read the right number.@k@vS14/03/210300100. Everyone must follow Rika-chan's rulings!@k@vS14/03/210300101. That way, nobody can complain. Sound fair?``
``Understood.@k@vS14/05/210500091. I wasn't feeling my best anyway, so the timing works out.``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ...Come to think of it, her complexion did look a bit off, backing up her words.
``Are you all right, Rika...?``
``I'll be fine just watching.@k@vS14/05/210500093. Satoko, you'll have to fight on in my place.``
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS14/04/210400109. Were I to fight hard enough to fill Rika's share, no human in the galaxy would be a match for me!``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: Satoko let out a boisterous, energetic laugh as she said that. Rika-chan clapped her hands together when she saw that.
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``Right!``
I don't quite understand the meaning of that... but regardless, that's some impressive self-confidence.
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: *pow*
""Yeaaaah——!!""
I see... so the qualification for entry in our club is to successfully break through our defenses? If that's the case, I'm glad to welcome any of the winners amongst our ranks!
changeReason: Minor rephrasing: 'stop them to prove ourselves' vs 'qualification for entry'; core meaning (proving worthiness through competition) is preserved.

TextENGNew: ``By the way, hugging and grabbing a person are considered valid forms of interference, but punching and kicking are strictly forbidden!@k@vS14/03/210300107. You got it, Rena?``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``Hauu~, why is Rena the only one being called out by name? By name?``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: Rena became sullen. But if she was able to use her punches freely, everyone would be KO'd and the game would end before we could even read any numbers out...
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``But that's a pretty strong handicap.@k@vS14/01/210100067. That may make this pretty hard.``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``Heh heh heh!@k@vS14/03/210300109. Kei-chan, is that all it takes to make you flinch?@k@vS14/03/210300110. For our club members, that sort of handicap is hardly a challenge!``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``Don't be ridiculous!@k@vS14/01/210100069. Don't you go slipping up and handing them victory on a silver platter either, Mion!``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``Aahaha, that's the spirit!@k@vS14/03/210300112. Let everyone know the true strength of our club, right down to the marrow of our bones!``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ""Ohhh!!""

Then, after confirming that all the club members were prepared to be ``it,`` and confirming that all the younger students had properly affixed the numbers to their clothes with safety pins, Mion shouted a proclamation.
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``Alright, let the game begin!@k@vS14/03/210300114. Start us up with a kickoff, Tomita-kun!``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: "Leave it to me!"
``Once the can has been returned here, we'll count to 100, so spread out!@k@vS14/03/210300116. Now, commence the battle!!!``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: On Mion's command, Tomita-kun took a running start and kicked the empty can sitting in the white circle with all his might.
Clang!! The can let out a satisfying sound as it sailed into the air, and at the same time, the younger students scattered with a cheer.
``Ahahahaha, that's an amazingly powerful kick!``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: As she said that, Rena immediately rushed over to where the can landed then returned it to the white circle in a flash. And with one foot on the can, she began counting: ``1, 2, 3, 4...``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: "Huh?@k@vS14/01/210100072. Come to think of it, isn't that the curry can from yesterday?@k@vS14/01/210100073. What happened to the contents—did you throw them out?"
"...I'd never do something that wasteful.@k@vS14/03/210300118. Chie-sensei ate it for lunch."

changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: Come to think of it, Chie-sensei seemed to be in a better mood than usual after lunch break. ...But wasn't that a commercial-size can large enough to feed 10 people?
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``She really is... the curry demon.``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: Wait, am I the only one who feels that's not the part to be pointing out? ...Not that it matters, I guess.
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.
TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: Meanwhile, Rena finished counting to 100, and we shifted into battle mode.
Rena stayed behind as the last line of defense, while Mion, Satoko, and I spread out along the back of the school building where the students would be hiding.
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: ``Now, where could they be...?``
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: I carefully surveyed my surroundings.
changeReason: TextENGNew appears misaligned -- translates different content than the JPN text.

TextENGNew: Unlike the previous zombie tag where you just had to touch someone, you had to confirm the number on their back. That meant reading their escape route and cornering them into a position where their back was visible.
changeReason: Minor perspective shift from first to second person ('I can get someone out' vs 'you just had to touch someone'); same gameplay mechanic described.

TextENGNew: Of course, you could also try to physically pin someone and force a look at their back. But if you found two or more at once, grappling with one meant the others could escape and target the can.
This special-rules kick the can... was remarkably tactical!
When I glanced toward some cover, I spotted two younger students wedged into a narrow gap. The moment our eyes met, they broke into a dead sprint toward the can.
Had they coordinated in advance? They split in different directions before converging on the can -- but the instant they turned their backs, I'd already memorized both their numbers!
``Whoooaaa!!!``
I blew past both of them in a flash and slammed my foot down on the can.
``...You're out.``

Rika-chan raised her hand and the two boys dropped to their knees in defeat. Before I could even catch my breath, three more students -- flushed out by Satoko -- came hurtling from behind the school building, heading straight for the can at my feet!
``Three eliminated.``
I repeated the numbers Satoko called out while stamping my foot on the can. Rika-chan raised her hand and declared all three out.
...So that was another valid tactic under these special rules.
The hound-role seeker flushes out the prey, and the hunter-role seeker finishes them off from beside the can.
A strategy only possible with multiple seekers -- but it required the hound to accurately read back numbers while chasing, or the hunter couldn't confirm the kills.
In other words, coordination was paramount -- and that was precisely where we club members held our greatest advantage.
I stepped out of the white circle before Rika-chan's five-second count expired, then immediately stepped back in and called the numbers with my foot on the can.
Next, a group driven out by Mion charged the can in a desperate comeback attempt.
And then, a few minutes later...
``All right, next!``
``...Wait, huh?@k@vS14/03/210300124. It's over already?``
Mion counted the captured students gathered near the can and came to a halt. Every single one of the younger students had been eliminated in no time flat.
``Hmm~~...@k@vS14/03/210300126. I was expecting a bit more of a fight...@k@vS14/03/210300127. I may have misjudged them.``
``Hey, come on, don't say it like that.``
``But this wasn't even a warm-up.``
Mion scratched her temple and let out an exasperated sigh right in front of the dejected younger students.
Looking over, neither Rena nor Satoko had broken a sweat or was even breathing hard. The same went for me, having spent most of the round stationed at the can as hunter.
``Hey, Mion?@k@vS14/01/210100085. How about we tweak the rules a bit to give the younger kids more of an advantage?``

``They already have plenty of advantages.@k@vS14/03/210300130. They don't have to call out names.@k@vS14/03/210300131. If the sides were reversed, we'd have used decoys and flanking maneuvers ages ago.``
Mion shot down my suggestion without mercy.
...She had a point -- if we were the hiders, we'd have used one or two people as decoys while a striker exploited the opening. I understood what she was getting at, but still...
``At this rate, nobody's making the cut...``
Reacting to our exchange, Okamura-kun -- who had been hanging his head until just now -- suddenly lifted his face with fierce determination.
``One more time!@k@vS14/25/212500010. Please, let us try again!``
On that cue, every younger student who'd been sitting on the ground rose to their feet.
``Everyone...!``
``Hoh... sure, why not.@k@vS14/03/210300134. But at your current level, the result will be the same no matter how many times you try~~.``
``Next time will be different!@k@vS14/25/212500012. Next time, it'll be a real fight!!``
With that declaration, Okamura-kun picked up the can and set it for kickoff. The younger students caught their breath and prepared to run.
``All right~, rematch it is!@k@vS14/03/210300136. Rika-chan, you're on!``
*CLANG*
On Rika-chan's signal, Okamura-kun launched the can skyward with a mighty kick. Once again, the younger students scattered behind the school building to hide.
``Now then, what strategy will they come up with this time...?``
Mion grinned like a predator ready to pounce, rotating her ankles in a lazy stretch.
As Rena placed the kicked can back in the circle and began counting with an air of total composure, Satoko cracked her knuckles beside her.

``Oh ho ho!@k@vS14/04/210400114. Okamura-san and the others must have realized by now that conventional kick-the-can tactics won't work.@k@vS14/04/210400115. They'll likely try... a mass rush, I'd wager.``
``Hehehe!@k@vS14/03/210300139. You've read it the same way, Satoko?@k@vS14/03/210300140. Then it's settled.@k@vS14/03/210300141. Rena, you take the vanguard. Kei-chan, back her up!``
``Hau, got it!``
``...Oh? Sure!``
I wasn't entirely sure what they meant, but Mion and Satoko had apparently read the younger students' strategy.
``But what kind of strategy will they--``
I glanced toward the school building. Sure enough, from both sides of the building, the younger students were visible -- making no effort to hide, clearly watching us.
``Aaaaaahhhh!!!``
The instant Rena's count reached 100, the younger students surged forward in a pack, launching a mass charge like a rugby scrum!
``I see...!@k@vS14/01/210100092. A brute-force numbers attack before we can read their backs!!``
``Heh heh heh... Good read on their part.``
``But -- that's still not enough!!``
True to Mion's words, Rena charged single-handedly into the oncoming wave of students!
Outnumbered, outgunned -- but those words simply did not exist in Rena's dictionary!!
*whoooosh*
``Hauuu~~, all these little ones trying so hard, they're sho kyuuute!@k@vS14/02/210200063. I wanna take them hooome~~!``
A display of instantaneous, godlike skill.
In the blink of an eye, the younger students were scattered across the ground, sitting there dumbfounded, not even sure what had happened.
Some of the girls had been scooped up in Rena's arms -- not her chameleon tongue, her arms -- and were receiving enthusiastic cheek-nuzzling from a Rena in full adorable mode.
``J-just now... she didn't use her Rena Punches, did she?``
``...Mii. I couldn't see clearly, but none of the fallen children appear bruised.@k@vS14/05/210500101. I rule that no strikes were used.``
With Rika-chan's judgment, Rena's takedowns were deemed valid. ...But seriously, Rena -- does she practice aikido or something?
``...Excellent work, Rena!@k@vS14/01/210100095. I bet you could snap chopsticks using just the paper wrapper!``
``Aah, that trick's a letdown, actually.@k@vS14/03/210300147. They just use a hidden finger to snap them.``
While we were talking, Tomita-kun and the other boys quickly recovered and got to their feet. But that momentary gap proved fatal against Satoko!
``You're out.``
Shot down the very instant they tried to stand and run, Tomita-kun and his group stood frozen in disbelief.
While Rena stopped the mass charge head-on, Satoko had circled behind them -- and when they turned their unguarded backs to get up, she picked them off!
``Oh, 691, 574, 164... heh heh heh, I can see everything!!``
The students who tried awkward maneuvers to avoid showing their backs to Satoko only ended up exposing their numbers to Mion, who was waiting by the can, and were eliminated effortlessly.
Meanwhile, the girls Rena had captured had already lost all will to resist, and Rena read their numbers to declare them out.
``Heh heh heh!@k@vS14/03/210300152. The plan was solid, but you can't overpower Rena with a frontal assault.@k@vS14/03/210300153. ...So, is that all you've got?``
``Ggh... Not yet!@k@vS14/24/212400012. It's not over yet!!``

Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun rose once more, clenching their fists. The other students, though shaken by the overwhelming gap in ability, followed their lead -- the will to join the club still burning strong.
``Ahahah, now that's the spirit!@k@vS14/03/210300155. Then I'll just keep crushing you until you're crying for mercy!!``
``H-hey, Mion...``
I started to say 'that's going too far,' but Mion cut me off by provocatively tapping the can with her toe inside the circle. The way she was openly belittling the younger students made me...
``Now, who's up for the next kickoff~?``
...Something felt off.

The Mion I'd known across all these timelines could be blunt with her words and sometimes kept people at arm's length, leading to misunderstandings -- but underneath, she was genuinely kind and considerate.
So even while she ran everyone ragged with her club activities, she always kept an eye on the younger students' wellbeing.
But this time... it was wrong. It looked like she was bullying them just to flaunt her own superiority. I had never seen Mion act like this.
``Could it be... Shion?@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1021. No... that doesn't seem right either.``
I dismissed the thought almost as soon as it arose.

True, Shion had occasionally infiltrated this school disguised as Mion in the past. But that was always to investigate Hinamizawa's or the Sonozaki's secrets -- and judging from yesterday, that didn't seem to be the case.
Besides, this version of Keiichi and Rena should be able to tell Mion and Shion apart easily.
...In which case, that really was Mion? The more I thought about it, the less I understood.
The whole new-member idea, too. Normally, Mion would have welcomed anyone who wanted to join without any test at all.

And when choosing games, she always put thought into making sure everyone could enjoy them equally, creating an environment where even beginners felt comfortable -- that was club president Sonozaki Mion.
``What on earth... is this world...?``
My unease crystallized into a question.
But there was no one to answer.
...No -- normally there had always been someone right here beside me. But now, the only response was the hollow sound of my own breath.
...I'm frightened.
A shudder ran through my entire body, though I wasn't cold at all.
The familiar and predictable had become tedious. ...Yes, that was how it used to be.
I used to look forward to small deviations. ...Yes, just a little while ago.
When it didn't rain on a day it should have, or when someone showed up who shouldn't have been there -- my heart would flutter with excitement.
After endlessly repeating thousands of cycles, those deviations that made my numbing daily existence interesting were my very reason for living.
And yet...
Even so...
Why am I so anxious right now?
Why am I so frightened, so lonely, so sad?
Even though I'm supposed to be a witch.
Even though I'm supposed to be a transcendent being who has lived for thousands of years, witnessed countless fates and the rise and fall of everything, and overcome even the fear of death itself--?
I asked the question. Over and over, inside my own heart.
Why? Why?
Why? Why? Why? Why? Why?
Why? Why? Why? Why? Why?
Why? Why? Why? Why? Why?
WHY? WHY? WHY? WHY? WHY...?
The question repeated like a broken record. ...But no answer ever came.
And then -- at last, I understood.
``...Ah, I see now...``
My heart was stable because there was always someone who showed me the way forward.
Like a bird navigating the sky, using the sun to find its way home.
Like a ship entering harbor, following the channel markers as it slowly glides forward.
I had... Hanyuu.
That was why I could feel at ease. That was why, even when I cried, or raged, or sank into despair... I could always find my way back to center.
But now, Hanyuu... was gone. And without her, I could no longer calmly process these changes, these unprecedented events.
Shion becoming gentle with Satoko.
Mion treating the younger students so coldly.
Before I could even savor the excitement of witnessing an unprecedented fate... the anxiety of not knowing what lay ahead would seize my heart first.
...How pathetic.
Was I always such a small, insignificant creature...?
``You're out.``

I called the out at Mion's prompting, keeping my expression carefully blank. Truthfully, I was struggling not to grimace from the revulsion welling up inside me. I didn't understand what Mion was thinking, but the irritation was impossible to suppress.
Mion's gleeful face as she called out number after number and shot down one student after another was truly insufferable. I just wanted this unpleasant time to end as quickly as possible...
``...Huh? Already over?``
After reading out the numbers Satoko fed her and confirming no one was left standing, Mion said that with a fearless grin.
Rena and Satoko both had some sweat on their brows and were breathing a bit harder, but they clearly had plenty of stamina left.
On the other hand, the aspiring club members...
``Haah, haah, haah... haah...!``
Finally, even Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun dropped to their knees.
Those two were still in relatively good shape. The others -- especially the younger girls -- were drenched in sweat, fighting back tears, and gasping for air.
My chest ached. Not the faint prickling from before -- this was a deep, gouging pain, so hot it felt ready to burst through my ribcage, rising like bile in my throat.
``Mii-chan, it's about time...``
``Hmm. So... what do you want to do?@k@vS14/03/210300162. Still want to keep going?``
``O-of course...!``
Okamura-kun and Tomita-kun said that and bravely got to their feet. But the others couldn't manage the same, and some had visibly given up, staring at the ground.
The frustration visible in their fleeting expressions and gestures. The helplessness. And above all -- the resignation born of defeat.
It must be frustrating.
It must be maddening.
More than hostility or hatred for an opponent... nothing makes a person's blood boil like realizing their own powerlessness.
And when you finally choose to surrender, to submit -- the self-loathing only deepens, dragging you into an ever-darker gloom...
Mion cut me off coldly without letting me finish. I bristled at her dismissive tone, but Mion remained unfazed, deflecting my glare with a cool expression.
``I understand how you feel, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300165. But if they want to be our comrades, they need the ability to back it up.``

``What our club members need is an indomitable spirit that never gives up, never breaks, and never falters!@k@vS14/03/210300167. So anyone who quits easily or can't give it their all until the very end -- we don't need them.``
``And failing to produce results is simply out of the question.@k@vS14/03/210300169. It's nonsensical.``
``...What the hell is that...?!``

``Well, Keiichi-san, you have been losing rather a lot lately, so it's not entirely surprising that you'd sympathize with the losers' perspective...@k@vS14/04/VT4Ba_satoko1015. But those are the standards.``

``Keiichi-kun.@k@vS14/02/210200066. ...Even if you let everyone join out of sympathy, if the skill gap is too wide, it'll eventually stop being fun for anyone.@k@vS14/02/210200067. Sometimes you have to be a little strict.``
``...Listen up!@k@vS14/00/teach10002. People who talk about 'relaxed education' and 'respecting individuality' think they know what they're talking about, but that's the whining of weaklings!``
``Winning the entrance exam war is far more important than meaning or fulfillment!@k@vS14/00/teach10004. Your childish ideas of kindness and equality are worthless in this competition!``
``Trample the hordes and pursue victory at all costs!@k@vS14/00/teach10006. You are--``
``That's how it is.@k@vS14/03/210300171. So, it's over.``
``All right, we're done~.@k@vS14/03/210300173. PE's almost over anyway, so let's cool down and--``
``Don't give me that BULLSHIT!!``
Finally, I raised my voice and turned on Mion and the others, rage laid bare.
Yes -- rage! The pain and discomfort that had been smoldering deep in my chest this whole time was anger. Anger at Mion and the others!
No need for people who can't give their all?
Out of the question if they can't produce results?
Games are only about winning? It's boring to just keep losing?!
Bullshit, bullshit, bullshit, BULLSHIT!!
What is this?! This is exactly the same as my old days back in Tokyo!!
Results are everything.
As long as you're one step above the rest, nothing else matters.
Obsessing over winning and losing, over superiority and inferiority, day in and day out, drowning in it--
Until eventually, I lost sight of why I was even fighting... I lost my sense of purpose, left dangling... and in the end, even the competition itself stopped being fun.
...No, my past doesn't matter. I've already left all of that behind.
But the one thing -- the very thing I refuse to accept, the thing I want to deny more than anything -- I cannot stand hearing it come from the mouths of my friends Mion, Rena, and Satoko!!
Because isn't that right?!
If someone can't give their all, you cheer them on until they can stand again. When they're about to break, you lend them your shoulder and teach them the joy of trying.
If they can't produce results, you stick with them until they do. If they want to give up, you lower the hurdle and show them the happiness of crossing the finish line.
And what if someone just can't win? That's the real nonsense! If one person can't win alone, then two! If that's not enough, then four! And then--!
``...The Hinamizawa tradition...``

``If one person has stones thrown at them, then two people throw stones back.@k@vS14/01/210100105. If two people have stones thrown at them, then four throw them back.@k@vS14/01/210100106. ...That's how it goes, right?``
``If a thousand people attack you, then all of Hinamizawa fights back together. ...That's how it goes, right?``
``Keiichi-kun...``
``That's the spirit of Hinamizawa!@k@vS14/01/210100110. So I've made my decision!@k@vS14/01/210100111. I'm going to make every last one of these kids our comrades!``
``And I'll keep teaching them -- the strength to never give up, and the joy of winning -- until they've truly claimed it for themselves!!!``
``K-Keiichi-sa--``
Satoko tried to interject, but Mion silenced her by stepping in front of me. Then, with a fearless grin, she spoke.
``Well, well, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300176. You've gotten awfully good at making big speeches.@k@vS14/03/210300177. My, my, I'm truly impressed!``

``...But you do understand, right?@k@vS14/03/210300180. It doesn't matter how passionate you get -- the three of us won't be convinced by words alone.@k@vS14/03/210300181. You know what you have to do?``
The way she said it -- for just one instant, one brief instant, I felt such fury and hatred toward the Mion standing before me that I could barely contain myself.
Was this really the Mion Sonozaki I believed was my greatest friend?! ...For a moment, the doubt and disappointment were so intense I nearly told her our friendship was over.
But I reconsidered. That wasn't the answer.
Staying away because you don't like someone. Cutting ties because they make you angry. ...All that proves is that you've admitted defeat and run away.
In that case -- there's only one option!
``...Yeah, I understand.@k@vS14/01/210100115. Our club rule... 'the winner is justice'!!@k@vS14/01/210100116. So I'll play your game -- and I'll beat you fair and square!!``
``Hoh... you've said it now, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300183. Just so you know, going up against us means you'd better be prepared for the consequences.``
``Of course!@k@vS14/01/210100118. Whatever penalty game you've got, bring it on!!@k@vS14/01/210100119. But in return -- when I win, you accept every single one of them as a new member! Agreed?!``
``Rewards and punishments will be honored.@k@vS14/03/210300185. If anyone other than the three of us manages to kick the can, it's your win!``
``...Of course, the chances of that happening are practically zero! Heh heh heh...!!``
``Don't you dare forget those words!@k@vS14/01/210100122. ...All right, everyone!!``
I spun around powerfully and faced the dejected younger students.
Every one of them looked utterly spent. Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun were at least still half-standing, but most of the others sat slumped on the ground, barely able to lift their heads.
But... I chose to rally them.
Because humans only truly lose when their spirit breaks!!

``How many of you can still move?@k@vS14/01/210100124. ...Don't hold back -- if you're too tired, rest! There's no shame in it!@k@vS14/01/210100125. But those who can still fight, even a little -- lend me your strength!``
``M-Maebara-san...``

``I know it's cruel to ask everyone who's exhausted to stand up one more time!@k@vS14/01/210100128. I know that! And I know the gap in ability is wide!@k@vS14/01/210100130. But all I need is one moment, one single opening -- and I promise I'll break through!``
``...But everyone, let me say this!!@k@vS14/01/210100132. I...``
I dropped to one knee... and spoke from the depths of my heart, pouring all my desire to encourage them into every word.
``I want to play club games with all of you!@k@vS14/01/210100134. So please, everyone -- lend me your strength, just this once!!``
``I want them to acknowledge you!@k@vS14/01/210100136. Your effort, your feelings, your spirit -- everything you've shown today!!``
``I refuse to let it end like this!@k@vS14/01/210100138. So just believe in me, even a little!@k@vS14/01/210100139. Even if victory seems impossible, this one last time -- trust me!``

``Think!@k@vS14/01/210100141. Think hard!@k@vS14/01/210100142. Your opponents are just a few years older -- that's all! They're not unbeatable monsters!@k@vS14/01/210100143. There's always a way to win -- if you use your heads!``
``...Everyone, have you always lost to Mion and Satoko in schoolwork?@k@vS14/01/210100144. That's not the case, is it?!``
``...I-I know the difference between cauliflower and broccoli!``
One of the girls who'd been hanging her head looked up and said that in a small voice. I took hold of her shoulders and roared 'That's the spirit!!' -- gently, yet powerfully.
``And there are only four opponents!@k@vS14/01/210100146. ...No, make that three now -- I'm one less on their side!@k@vS14/01/210100147. And of those three, only one actually stays by the can!``

``So you really only need to worry about two people!!@k@vS14/01/210100149. ...You see it now, Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun!@k@vS14/01/210100150. If you two can hold off those two, the rest can focus on the can!``
``B-but... against Ryuuguu-san...``
``You're right. Rena is certainly a threat.@k@vS14/01/210100152. ...That's why I'll stop Rena myself.@k@vS14/01/210100153. If I do that, you two just have to handle Satoko!``

Hearing that, the two boys' faces -- which had been on the verge of losing hope -- suddenly brightened. And following their lead, the other students gradually raised their heads and began watching me intently.
...Good, just a little more!
``Listen, everyone! You don't need to 'run' anymore!@k@vS14/01/210100155. Tomita-kun, Okamura-kun, and I will hold off the two hounds!``

``So the rest of you have one single mission -- aim for the can!!@k@vS14/01/210100157. Don't fear Mion's tag!@k@vS14/01/210100158. Straight for the can -- just walk if you have to! One kick is all it takes!``
``R-right!!!``
``All right, everyone...``
I held out my hand. Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun were the first to place theirs on top. Then, one by one, every student piled their small but determined hands onto ours.
``This is our last chance.@k@vS14/01/210100161. ...Everyone, let's go!!@k@vS14/01/210100162. Final battle!!!``
``YEAAAHH!!!``
...Incredible.
I could scarcely believe what I was witnessing.
``...It can't be...!``
Just moments ago, every single one of those younger students had been utterly demoralized.

They wouldn't have said it outright, but exhaustion and helplessness had drained them of the will to even stand. They wore expressions of someone who had half -- no, almost entirely -- given up on joining the club.
And yet, look at them now.

A few words from Keiichi, and they were surging with fighting spirit equal to -- or perhaps even greater than -- what they'd had at the very start. They hauled themselves up, brushed the dirt from their clothes, and stood tall.
To be honest... I had thought they looked pathetic.
Hitting a minor obstacle and crumbling.
Refusing to accept defeat, refusing to face reality, keeping their heads down, passively waiting for someone to offer sympathy -- such a servile attitude.
Perhaps that wasn't actually the case. But that was all I could see.
Mion's obnoxious grandstanding was bad enough to watch, but honestly, witnessing those younger students had been just as grating.
So I had turned my gaze inward... stopped thinking, and resolved to simply stand still until time ran out.
...But Keiichi was different.
He believed in their potential, and chose to reach out to them -- even if it meant opposing his own friends.
It wasn't the condescending 'charity' of a superior. It was nothing like cheap sympathy... It was a gentle yet powerful encouragement of the heart.
And so everyone stood up again.
Of their own free will, unforced, they reclaimed the will to fight.
``As expected... that's what makes you Keiichi.``
That never changes.
Keiichi alone -- in every timeline, in every circumstance -- never changes.
His fierce convictions have sometimes spun out of control, run wild... and led to tragedy. Even so, Keiichi fought against fate until the very, very end.
And because of that strength -- the people around him simply cannot look away. That is what I truly believe, from the bottom of my heart.
``...Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1029. Will you be the one to show it to me?@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1030. Something to replace this unpleasant feeling... with something bright?``
Before my eyes, Keiichi was setting up the can for kickoff.
Watching him, I noticed with faint surprise that my heart had begun to lift.
``All right, here we go!@k@vS14/01/210100164. Yaaaaaah!!``
*CLAAANG*
The empty can soared through the air with a satisfying ring. Simultaneously, the younger students bolted behind the school building, and Rena and Satoko moved to give chase--
``Hold on, Rena, Satoko!``
``Wh-what is it?!``
``You can skip the countdown after retrieving the can.@k@vS14/01/210100167. But instead, you have to solve this problem before you go get it!``
``Wh-what could it be? What could it be?``

``Here's the question!@k@vS14/01/210100169. There's a bacterium that splits in two every minute.@k@vS14/01/210100170. If you put it in a bottle, it fills the bottle in one hour. How long would it take to fill the bottle if you started with two bacteria?``
``H-huh, w-well...!``
``Wh-what kind of bizarre question is that?!``
``Heh heh heh... Kei-chan, one of your classic trick riddles?@k@vS14/03/210300188. Sorry, but this old man won't fall for it!``
``The answer is -- obviously, half of one hour! 30 minutes!!``
``Wrong.@k@vS14/01/210100173. ...So you're not allowed to retrieve the can until you solve it.``
``Huh? Huuuh? W-why??@k@vS14/03/210300191. There are twice as many so it takes half as long, right?!``
``Rika-chan, keep watch on them until they get the right answer, okay?``
``Roger. Nipah~.``
Rika-chan waved her hand with an amused smile. ...I had already told her the answer, by the way.
``U-umm...@k@vS14/03/210300193. One hour is 60 minutes, so doubling the speed would make it 30 minutes... right?``
``H-hau~!@k@vS14/02/210200073. Rena's terrible at math~!``
``H-hold on, Rika!@k@vS14/04/210400124. Stop laughing and at least give us a hint!``
``The referee must remain impartial. Nipah~.``
...Behind me, Mion and the others were still locked in heated debate. This was buying us far more time than a simple count to 100.
I sprinted behind the school building. Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun were waiting there.
``Keiichi-san, preparations complete!``
``All the girls are in the classroom.@k@vS14/25/212500019. The remaining boys went to the other side of the building!``
``Good... timing is everything here!@k@vS14/01/210100176. Are you two all set?``
``Ready to go!!``
They flashed simultaneous thumbs-up with matching grins. Seeing that, I gave a firm nod and clasped their hands.
``All right!@k@vS14/01/210100178. From this moment on, we're allies!@k@vS14/01/210100179. Don't call me Maebara -- call me Keiichi!``
``Yes, Keiichi-san!@k@vS14/24/212400023. Please call me Daiki too!!``
``And me, Suguru!!@k@vS14/25/212500022. Keiichi-san, I'm putting everything in your hands!!``
They gripped my hand tightly with both of theirs. I placed my other hand on top and answered with a powerful 'Right!!'
Peeking at Mion's group from behind the building, I saw they'd finally solved it -- Rena was carrying the can back to the circle.
Rena looked like steam was practically rising from her head... and Satoko's face, visible even from here, was beet red.
As for Mion... she seemed to be wearing a wry smile. But her eyes were fixed steadily in my direction, unmoving. She almost certainly knew I was here, peering out at them.
All right... game on! Club members!!
``All forces--``
I slowly raised my hand -- then thrust it forward and roared!
``CHAAAARGE!!!``
``AAAAAHHHH!!``

With a war cry, our main force and the diversionary squad charged the can simultaneously. Moving to intercept, Rena and Satoko closed the distance in a flash -- Rena attacked the diversionary squad first!
``Here they come, here they come!@k@vS14/02/210200075. Ahahaha, rushing in all together won't help! Useless, useless, useless!!``
*pow* *pow* *pow*
The diversionary squad was bowled over in the blink of an eye by Rena's superhuman speed. And as they groaned and tried to get up, Satoko circled behind them to read their numbers!
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS14/04/210400126. A mass charge? How utterly unimaginative...``
``W-what's wrong, Satoko?!@k@vS14/03/210300195. Get their numbers, quick!``
Rika-chan crossed her arms in an X before her chest. Seeing that, a flustered Satoko misread several more numbers, all rejected by Rika-chan.
``W-what's wrong, Satoko-chan?@k@vS14/02/210200077. You have to read them correctly, okay? Okay?``
``W-well!@k@vS14/04/VT4Ba_satoko1017. Th-the numbers on their backs are upside down~~?!``
``Huuuuh?!``
Yes -- 'one' of the reasons for buying that extra time was to have everyone flip their bibs upside down!!
``K-Kei-chan... you really pulled a fast one~!@k@vS14/03/210300197. ...Referee!!``
``Haha!@k@vS14/01/210100183. Nobody said which direction the numbers had to face.@k@vS14/01/210100184. ...Right, Rika-chan?``
``...Permitted.@k@vS14/05/210500119. No violation.``

Rika-chan beamed and formed a large circle above her head with her arms. In the meantime, several members of the diversionary squad who'd escaped Satoko's calls fully recovered and launched another assault on the can!!
``R-Rena-saaan!!``
``...Ahahaha!@k@vS14/02/210200080. Not a chance!!``
``You're not taking another step forward!!``
*pow* *pow* *pow*
Hit by Rena's near-divine speed, the diversionary squad went down again. But this time, Rena added a twist -- literally -- flipping them face-down instead of face-up?!
From Satoko's viewpoint, the face-down students were effectively doing handstands -- and their inverted numbers were now right-side-up and perfectly legible!
``Ahahaha!@k@vS14/02/210200083. Well played, Keiichi-kun -- interesting strategy!@k@vS14/02/210200084. You had me worried for just a teensy moment!``
``Oh ho ho ho!!@k@vS14/04/210400136. In the end, it was nothing more than a half-baked trick!!``

Having wiped out the diversionary squad, Rena and Satoko immediately moved to block our main force. They darted left and right, looking for openings to get behind us. And of course, Mion the hunter had eyes on everything from her position by the can.
It was like a dogfight against two fighters while dodging long-range bombardment from a battleship. Worthy opponents, as expected from club members. ...But! That was all accounted for!!
``Rena!@k@vS14/01/210100186. I'm your opponent!``
``Ahahaha!@k@vS14/02/210200086. Sorry, but the way Rena is now, not even Keiichi-kun can stop me.@k@vS14/02/210200087. I'll get past you in one step!``
``Oh, bring it!@k@vS14/01/210100188. ...Daiki! Suguru! Satoko's yours!``
``Roger!``
``Leave it to us!``
The two boys immediately began sidling away to draw Satoko from me. ...Their baseball training showed -- their lateral movement was impressively smooth.
Satoko tried to dart behind them for an instant, but they immediately pressed back-to-back, shielding each other's numbers.
``S-such cowardice!@k@vS14/04/210400139. Rika?!``
``...No rules were specified about concealment methods.@k@vS14/05/210500121. No violation.``

Overruled by Rika-chan, Satoko had no choice but to try grabbing them. But the physical size difference worked against her, and she was gradually being pushed back, allowing them to inch closer to the can.
``Rena-saaan! Stop those two!!``
``Got it!@k@vS14/02/210200089. I'm on my way-- huh?!``
``Nuh-uh!@k@vS14/01/210100190. Didn't you say I was your opponent?``
Seeing me feint a dash toward the can, Rena held her ground instead of bolting away.
Then, apparently deciding I was her true target, the smile vanished from her face. She turned to face me with an expressionless, razor-sharp gaze.
``Keiichi-kun...@k@vS14/02/210200092. If you keep getting in the way, Rena might get a little... angry.``
``That sounds interesting.@k@vS14/01/210100192. Angry Rena is 'pretty' too -- I want to burn that image into my memory!``
``So... you've chosen this, then?@k@vS14/02/210200094. Then... shall we?``
A shiver...!!
Rena's eyes sharpened to a hawkish amber. The ferocious pressure locked my feet -- no, my entire body -- in place, as if frozen solid.
A piercing gaze, sharp enough to pin a person where they stood. ...And then--
Almost purely on reflex, I backstepped and dodged by a hair's breadth the arms that lunged for my ribs and chest. Glancing down at my gym shirt collar, one of the buttons was already gone.
``Well done.@k@vS14/02/210200096. I was sure I had you.``
``But what about next time...?``
Rena advanced step by step, closing the distance. The crushing pressure nearly drove me backward... but I held my ground at the last moment.
As I watched Rena, I realized it was the sharpness of her gaze that had been throwing me off.
When I widened my field of vision to take in her whole body, I could see her movements, her bearing -- and the fact that she, like me, was breathing hard, her shoulders rising and falling.
...That's it -- her eyes! As long as I don't look into her eyes, I can track her movements just fine!
Of course, I couldn't let my own uncertainty show... so I kept my focus soft and broad, maintaining a composed stance.
Rena was feeling the same anxiety I was. The more evenly matched we were, the more any movement became a potential opening -- making both of us hesitate to strike.
In a standoff like this, the optimal strategy was to watch for the opponent's move and counter. The one who acted first was at a disadvantage.
But unlike me, Rena was under time pressure -- she needed to go help Satoko against Daiki and Suguru.
That impatience, clashing with her caution, would eventually force her into an aggressive breakthrough -- and that would be my greatest opportunity!
The next instant, I felt Rena reach for my left sleeve. I sidestepped right with a small backstep and evaded it.
Then, as she brushed past me, I hooked my foot around her extended left leg and gave her shoulder a light tap with the spin's momentum.
``Kyah?!``
Caught off guard by the counter, Rena stumbled and landed on her rear. I almost reached out to help her up, but caught myself just in time, remembering the situation.
``Hau~~, Keiichi-kun, that's mean...!``
``S-sorry!@k@vS14/01/210100197. I-I honestly didn't expect you to fall...!``
``Ahh~, that was cruel, Kei-chan~.@k@vS14/03/210300199. You'd better brace yourself for one hell of a penalty game.``
I suddenly realized that Mion -- who was supposed to be guarding the can -- was standing right here. Whipping around, I looked past her shoulder and saw Daiki and Suguru on their knees in defeat...!
``Well... you certainly entertained us.@k@vS14/03/210300201. But it's time to end this game!``
``Oh ho ho!@k@vS14/04/210400142. Impressive effort, Keiichi-san... but you simply didn't have enough pieces on the board!``
Satoko advanced with a fearless grin, closing in to surround me. Before I knew it, Rena had risen and was circling behind me to cut off my retreat.
If this continued, one of them would read my number, and the other two would pin me down while Mion ran back to the can. Game over.
``M-Mion, are you sure it's wise to leave the base unguarded...?@k@vS14/01/210100201. There should still be soldiers out there.``
``Aahahaha!@k@vS14/03/210300203. Don't underestimate this old man.@k@vS14/03/210300204. The remaining girls don't even have the energy to run anymore.``
I see... Mion had foreseen even that...

``I have to hand it to you, though -- that quiz earlier was a real curveball.@k@vS14/03/210300206. We couldn't make heads or tails of it for a while, and it cost us a lot of time.@k@vS14/03/210300207. The answer is 59 minutes, right?``
``...Out of curiosity, who figured it out?``
``Ahahaha, it was Rena.@k@vS14/02/210200101. But even after I explained it, Mii-chan couldn't wrap her head around it for a while.``
...I see. That's Mion for you...
``Now then... are you prepared?@k@vS14/03/210300209. Penalty game included.``
``Sure.@k@vS14/01/210100205. ...But are you okay with that?``
``Those younger students heading for the can over there.``
``Not this trick again~!@k@vS14/03/210300212. Didn't I tell you?@k@vS14/03/210300213. Those kids don't have the energy to run anymore.``
``Sure... but it looks like they might have the energy to walk.``
At that, Mion spun around -- and gaped at what she saw: the younger girls, slowly but surely making their way toward the can.
...Yes! This was our final hidden ace!!
While our main force drew all three opponents away, the girls had rested, recovered their strength, undergone a certain 'preparation,' and now attacked the unguarded base as a guerrilla squad!!
Running was obviously beyond them -- but I'd confirmed beforehand that a little rest would be enough for them to at least walk!
``S-Satoko!!``
``R-roger!!``
Satoko pivoted and dashed to intercept the girls' advance.
``...But Kei-chan, should you really have revealed that~?@k@vS14/03/210300217. You just told us about your surprise attack.``
``Did I?@k@vS14/01/210100209. ...Rena, take a good look at those kids.``
``H-hauuuu?!``
The instant Rena saw the girls toddling forward, she burst -- blood gushing from her nose as she recoiled. During their rest, the girls had changed out of their gym clothes and into costumes!
School swimsuits, maid outfits, waitress uniforms -- and every single one of them wearing cat ears as default!!
They were squeezing out the last drops of their stamina, swaying left and right as they walked... looking exactly like adorable little pets!!
``I-I waaannaa t-t-take them h-hooooome with meeeeeee~~~!!!!``
``R-R-Rena?!@k@vS14/03/210300219. W-wait! N-no matter how you look at it, this is obviously a trap~~?!``
Unable to resist their adorable allure, Rena completely forgot she was supposed to be surrounding me and hurtled off toward the girls like a shot.
...And of course, I wasn't naive enough to let that opening go to waste!!
``WHOOOAAAA!!!``
``Oh no?!``
``Wait, Rena! Satoko!@k@vS14/03/210300222. S-stop Kei-chan!!``
No longer bothering to hide my back, I slipped past Mion and broke into a dead sprint toward the can!
Mion the hunter had abandoned her post to pursue me... which meant enemy territory was wide open!! This was my true objective all along!!!
Satoko spotted me, and Rena finally snapped out of her trance. Both frantically raced back toward the can.
Speed equal... distance equal...! Then the deciding factor was -- sheer willpower!!
``UOOOOOHHH!!!``
Ten meters left...
``Stop him!! STOP HIM!!!``
Five meters left...
``G-gotta make iiiiit!!``
Three meters left...
``Y-you will NOT reach the goooal!!!``
Then...
``RAAAAAAGH!!!``
The dust settled.
I pushed myself up from the sliding dive and looked ahead.
The can......... was still there.
Satoko's desperate sliding kick.
Rena's arm, stretched to its absolute limit in a last-ditch save.
My strike -- carrying the hopes of everyone -- had been stopped by a razor-thin margin.
My voice wouldn't come.
My body wouldn't move.
And the same was true for Rena, Satoko... and surely Mion, somewhere behind us.
Nobody could move... we all just stared at the can in silence.
I... lost?
After rallying everyone like that.
After filling them with hope and dreams... after all those bold declarations.
In the end...
In the end, I...!
Then, with light, unhurried footsteps--
Rika-chan walked up to the can and raised her right hand.
And then.
``...The winner is--``
*clang*
``Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500124. ...and the younger students' team.``
Right before our eyes... Rika-chan kicked the can herself.
She turned to me as I stood there, stunned -- and smiled.
``R-Rika-chan...?``
``Congratulations, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500126. You... won.``
``Ah... no, I mean... B-but--?``
``'If anyone other than the three of us kicks the can, it's Keiichi's team's win' -- that's what Mii said.``
``So nobody can object if I kicked it.@k@vS14/05/210500129. ...Isn't that right, Mii?``
Mion grimaced at her own oversight. But the expression quickly softened into a rueful chuckle, and then into a warm, genuine smile.
``Congratulations, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/210300227. ...You win.``
``W-we did iiiiit!!!``
Following Rika-chan's declaration and Mion's acknowledgment--
All the boys who had been doubled over in frustration moments ago, Daiki and Suguru among them--
All the girls who had collapsed to the ground, too spent to stand--
All at once--
With jubilant cries, muddy sweat, and warm tears--
They rushed toward me... each lifting their voice in their own expression of joy.
Just then, from far away, the principal's end-of-school chime rang out.
Somehow, it seemed to ring louder and more triumphantly than usual... as if celebrating our victory.
